title: dianthus lactiflorus
intro: Dianthus lactiflorus, commonly known as the milk-pink or white- flowering pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dianthus lactiflorus include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Dianthus lactiflorus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
